title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Cluster Case: K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary
summary: Platform and application teams must share external HTTP routing without annotation-specific coupling, cross-namespace privilege, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s ingress gateway boundary increment in Gateway API routing and delegation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.
Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary · Theory K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries. A defensible solution applies K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to Gateway API routing and delegation policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-ingress-gateway-boundary — Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-5
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: worked k8s gateway ingress routing tls 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-ingress-gateway-boundary-kjzzq1
  - competency: k8s-ingress-gateway-boundary
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: ingress gateway boundary
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: kjzzq1
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
